name: Basic Pita
recipeIngredient: 5 to 5 1/2 cups unbleached all-purpose flour, plus flour for kneading
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the water into a large bowl and sprinkle in the yeast. Stir until dissolved.
2. Add sugar and salt to the yeast mixture. Mix thoroughly with a wooden spoon.
3. Gradually add 5 cups of flour, stirring constantly until smooth. Knead with hands until no longer sticky.
4. Turn dough onto a well-floured board. Knead until smooth and elastic (about 5 minutes).
5. Shape dough into an even rectangle and cut in half lengthwise. Divide each half into 12 portions for small pitas, or 6 for larger ones.
6. Form each portion into a smooth ball. Place on a floured surface.
7. Flour work surfaces and rolling pin. Roll each round from the center to the outside edge, giving dough a quarter turn after each roll until forming a perfect circle (not quite 1/4-inch thick).
8. For small pitas: about 5-5 1/2 inches in diameter; for large pitas: about 8-1/2 inches.
9. Flip circles to smooth out creases that might prevent pockets from forming.
10. Preheat oven to 500F and place an ungreased baking sheet inside.
11. As each pita is rolled, place it on a floured surface covered with a clean, dry towel. Let rise in a warm, draft-free area for 30 to 45 minutes.
12. Bake small pitas (4) or one large pita on the bottom rack until puffed and lightly browned on the bottom, almost white on top (about 4 minutes). For large pita: about 3-1/2 minutes.
13. If desired, flip loaves over after puffing and bake for up to 1 minute longer to brown tops. Be careful not to crisp or brittle.
14. Remove hot pitas from oven and wrap in clean, dry towels until cool enough to handle. Serve warm or at room temperature.
15. Makes 24 small pitas or 12 large.
From "Pita the Great"
San Francisco Chronicle, 8/10/88
Stephen Ceideberg, November 1992
